summary: leo-logger ships binding.gyp with command-expansion that runs arbitrary code during install
details: The package's binding.gyp contains GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)` / `<!@(...)`) at line 6 inside a sources/targets field. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even with no declared install/postinstall script — and GYP evaluates these expressions as shell commands during the configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute on every `npm install` of this package, functionally equivalent to a malicious lifecycle hook. The package presents itself as a 'logger' but ships no native C/C++ source that would justify a node-gyp build configuration, so the binding.gyp's only effective purpose is to trigger command execution at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
